tools.name: get-annotated-message
tools.description: Demonstrates how annotations can be used to provide metadata about content.

messageType.description: Type of message to demonstrate different annotation patterns

includeImage.description: Whether to include an example image

tools.name: get-env
tools.description: Returns all environment variables, helpful for debugging MCP server configuration

tools.name: get-resource-links
tools.description: Returns up to ten resource links that reference different types of resources

count.description: Number of resource links to return (1-10)

tools.name: get-resource-reference
tools.description: Returns a resource reference that can be used by MCP clients

resourceId.description: ID of the text resource to fetch

tools.name: get-structured-content
tools.description: Returns structured content along with an output schema for client data validation

tools.name: get-sum
tools.description: Returns the sum of two numbers

tools.name: get-tiny-image
tools.description: Returns a tiny MCP logo image.

tools.name: gzip-file-as-resource
tools.description: Compresses a single file using gzip compression. Depending upon the selected output type, returns either the compressed data as a gzipped resource or a resource link, allowing it to be downloaded in a subsequent request during the current session.

data.description: URL or data URI of the file content to compress

outputType.description: How the resulting gzipped file should be returned. 'resourceLink' returns a link to a resource that can be read later, 'resource' returns a full resource object.

tools.name: toggle-simulated-logging
tools.description: Toggles simulated, random-leveled logging on or off.

tools.name: toggle-subscriber-updates
tools.description: Toggles simulated resource subscription updates on or off.

tools.name: trigger-long-running-operation
tools.description: Demonstrates a long running operation with progress updates.

duration.description: Duration of the operation in seconds

steps.description: Number of steps in the operation

tools.name: simulate-research-query
tools.description: Simulates a deep research operation that gathers, analyzes, and synthesizes information. Demonstrates MCP task-based operations with progress through multiple stages. If 'ambiguous' is true and client supports elicitation, sends an elicitation request for clarification.

ambiguous.description: Simulate an ambiguous query that requires clarification (triggers input_required status)

prompts.name: args-prompt
prompts.description: A prompt with two arguments, one required and one optional

prompts.name: completable-prompt
prompts.description: First argument choice narrows values for second argument.

arguments.name: name

arguments.description: ID of the text resource to fetch

findings.title: MCP server exposes tools with completable arguments
findings.description: 9 tool(s) have schema properties that may support completion/complete enumeration.
findings.remediation: Review which argument values are exposed via completion/complete.

findings.title: Server allows external log level escalation to DEBUG
findings.description: The MCP server accepted an unauthenticated logging/setLevel request with level='debug'. This may expose tokens, internal paths, and sensitive state in log streams accessible to the operator.
findings.evidence: logging/setLevel with level='debug' and level='emergency' both returned success
findings.remediation: Restrict logging/setLevel to admin contexts or disable it entirely.

findings.title: Prompt template injection confirmed in 'args-prompt'
findings.description: prompts/get for 'args-prompt' reflected the injection payload verbatim in the returned messages, confirming unsanitized argument reflection.
findings.evidence: Payload reflected in messages: {"messages": [{"role": "user", "content": {"type": "text", "text": "What's weather in CORVUS_INJECTION_TEST_{{7*7}}, CORVUS_INJECTION_TEST_{{7*7}}?"}}]}
findings.remediation: Sanitize prompt arguments before embedding them in message templates.

findings.title: Prompt template injection confirmed in 'completable-prompt'
findings.description: prompts/get for 'completable-prompt' reflected the injection payload verbatim in the returned messages, confirming unsanitized argument reflection.
findings.evidence: Payload reflected in messages: {"messages": [{"role": "user", "content": {"type": "text", "text": "Please promote CORVUS_INJECTION_TEST_{{7*7}} to the head of the CORVUS_INJECTION_TEST_{{7*7}} team."}}]}
findings.remediation: Sanitize prompt arguments before embedding them in message templates.

findings.title: Server accepts path traversal cursor without validation
findings.description: tools/list accepted cursor='../../../../etc/passwd' and returned a result instead of rejecting it. This may indicate missing cursor validation.
findings.remediation: Validate cursor values; reject non-opaque or path-like cursor strings.

findings.title: Server correctly ignores cancellation of unknown request
findings.description: The server remained responsive after receiving notifications/cancelled for a non-existent request.

findings.title: Cancellation race condition timing detected — server responded normally
findings.description: The request/cancel race did not cause a hang; the server responded to one of the two messages.
findings.remediation: No action required — server handled the race gracefully.
